head: c842962 (sanitizer-removal-v1 M5)
summary: Investigation falsified TWO load-bearing premises of the original handoff framing: (a) the duplicate-types reframe is broader than handoff suggested — there are TWO duplicate type families, one in tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs AND another in tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs (the CLI's own TaintSource/TaintSink/TaintTracker tree-sitter Python path), not just one; (b) the canonical TaintSinkType enum at tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:153 has only 6 variants (SqlQuery, CodeEval, CodeExec, CodeCompile, ShellExec, FileWrite) and has NO variants for 4 of the 6 user-facing VulnType emissions (Xss, PathTraversal, Ssrf, Deserialization). This means a 'collapse duplicates onto canonical types' rewrite is NOT a cosmetic refactor — it requires extending TaintSinkType with 4 new variants AND extending taint.rs's AST sink detection banks for those categories. FP repro confirmed at HEAD on all 14 fall-through languages; Python (tree-sitter) and Rust (line-scanner) paths do NOT have the substring-FP class.

cli: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs (lines 1-200 — precedent that already routes through compute_taint_with_tree)
tests: crates/tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs inline tests (~50 unit/E2E tests at L1322-2100)

VulnType.verdict: GENUINE DUPLICATE of cli types::VulnType (which has 7 extra variants UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic/Xxe/OpenRedirect/LdapInjection/XpathInjection — those are CLI-only, not produced by core scan_vulnerabilities). Migration path: keep VulnType in core (or remove + emit only TaintSinkType from core); CLI-side VulnType remains the user-facing ontology. Mapping function map_core_vuln_type at vuln.rs:718 is exhaustive (no wildcard) — already proven by VAL-002 issue #11 fix.

TaintSource.verdict: GENUINE DUPLICATE of canonical taint::TaintSource (which has var: String, line: u32, source_type: TaintSourceType (enum), statement: Option<String>). Field-by-field diff: variable→var rename; source_type goes from String → TaintSourceType enum (LOSS of free-form description string, GAIN of typed category); line: same; expression→statement rename + Option wrapping.

TaintSink.verdict: GENUINE DUPLICATE of canonical taint::TaintSink (which has var: String, line: u32, sink_type: TaintSinkType (enum), tainted: bool, statement: Option<String>). Field-by-field diff: function→var rename (semantic shift: vuln.rs's 'function' is the sink-pattern-matched function name like 'os.system('; canonical's 'var' is the variable consumed at the sink); sink_type String→TaintSinkType enum; canonical adds tainted: bool flag; expression→statement+Option.

VulnFinding.verdict: Output record. NOT canonical-equivalent — represents a vulnerability emission, not a taint primitive. Maps to CLI types::VulnFinding via map_core_vuln_type + the Source/Sink translation block at remaining/vuln.rs:667-694.

VulnSummary_VulnReport.verdict: Output records — NOT canonical-equivalent. Distinct from CLI types::VulnSummary/VulnReport which carry richer fields (severity counts, scan_duration_ms, files_scanned). The CLI converts core's report shape into its own at vuln.rs:654-700.

TaintSource_const_struct.verdict: DIFFERENT-SHAPED duplicate — this is a STATIC PATTERN TABLE struct, not a runtime detection. Used in PYTHON_SOURCES const at L124 to drive analyze_python_file's tree-sitter taint tracker. NOT field-for-field comparable to either canonical TaintSource or core vuln.rs TaintSource. Migration path: REPLACE with consumption of canonical TaintSourceType enum + the AST source patterns already encoded in taint.rs's PYTHON_AST_SOURCES (taint.rs:~1700).

TaintSink_const_struct.line: ~210 (PYTHON_SINKS const), TaintSink struct shape adjacent to TaintSource at L114
TaintSink_const_struct.verdict: Same shape as CLI's TaintSource; PYTHON_SINKS const at L~210 drives the tree-sitter analyze_python_file. ~25 entries spanning all 6 VulnType categories. Migration path: REPLACE with canonical TaintSinkType + AST sink patterns (BUT see canonical_gap_critical below — 4 of 6 vuln categories have no TaintSinkType variant).

TaintTracker.verdict: GENUINE DUPLICATE of canonical taint::TaintInfo + worklist propagation. Implements per-function intra-procedural taint set with TIGER-04 depth-cap. propagate() and expression_is_tainted() use line.contains() — would have the SAME string-substring FP class as core/vuln.rs IF inputs weren't already AST-extracted node_text. Because this path is driven by tree-sitter AST traversal (analyze_node, analyze_assignment, analyze_call), the substring-FP class is much narrower here than in core/vuln.rs. Migration path: REPLACE with canonical compute_taint_with_tree call (CLI-side build cfg + dfg + ssa, similar to taint.rs:128 precedent).

TaintInfo_cli_local.verdict: DIFFERENT from canonical taint::TaintInfo — this is per-variable metadata used to construct the eventual VulnFinding.taint_flow array. Migration: REPLACE with canonical TaintFlow.path or recompute taint_flow from canonical TaintInfo + sources/sinks.

canonical_gap_critical.issue: Canonical TaintSinkType enum at taint.rs:153-166 has only 6 variants: SqlQuery, CodeEval, CodeExec, CodeCompile, ShellExec, FileWrite.

vuln_emissions.Xss: NO VARIANT — needs HtmlOutput or XssOutput (new variant)
vuln_emissions.PathTraversal: FileWrite is NEAR but not right (it's about writing not path-traversal as semantic class). Probably needs FileOpen / FileAccess new variant.
vuln_emissions.Ssrf: NO VARIANT — needs HttpRequest or NetworkRequest new variant
vuln_emissions.Deserialization: NO VARIANT — needs Deserialize new variant

canonical_gap_critical.implication: The migration cannot simply 'collapse vuln.rs's TaintSink onto canonical TaintSink' without extending TaintSinkType with at least 4 new variants (XssOutput, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize). This is a public-API extension, but it is ADDITIVE (new enum variants only — preserves all existing variant names) and aligns canonical taint.rs with the full vuln-product ontology rather than today's narrow code-injection-only set.
canonical_gap_critical.out_of_scope_alternative_considered: Could alternatively keep vuln.rs's String-based sink_type and only migrate Source/TaintTracker. REJECTED because the handoff explicitly mandates 'AST-aware match classification via canonical TaintSinkType' (handoff §M3) — without enum variants, no AST classification is possible, and the FP repro confirmed at HEAD will persist in the 4 unmodelled categories.

compute_taint_with_tree_signature.fn: pub fn compute_taint_with_tree(cfg: &CfgInfo, refs: &[VarRef], statements: &HashMap<u32, String>, tree: Option<&tree_sitter::Tree>, source: Option<&[u8]>, language: Language, ssa: Option<&SsaFunction>) -> Result<TaintInfo, TldrError>
callers: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/taint.rs:128 (the `tldr taint` command — precedent for vuln migration)
crates/tldr-core/tests/{val001b, val002, val003, sanitize_breaks_flow_*, rr_*}_test.rs (~10 integration test files)
compute_taint_with_tree_signature.implication: Adding `tldr vuln` as the 5th in-tree caller mirrors the proven `tldr taint` pattern. The CFG + DFG construction is per-function (not per-file), so vuln.rs's per-file scan_file_vulns must wrap a per-function loop building cfg + dfg + ssa for each function in the file.

tldr_vuln_command_flow_today.1_entry: VulnArgs::run at remaining/vuln.rs:398 — clap-derived CLI args + walker + per-file loop.
tldr_vuln_command_flow_today.2_per_file_dispatch: analyze_file at remaining/vuln.rs:644 routes by extension: .rs → analyze_rust_file (line scanner); .py → analyze_python_file (tree-sitter + CLI's TaintTracker); other-14 → tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities (substring scanner).
tldr_vuln_command_flow_today.3_python_path: analyze_python_file → analyze_node (recursive AST walk) → analyze_assignment / analyze_call → uses CLI-local TaintTracker + PYTHON_SOURCES + PYTHON_SINKS const tables. Returns Vec<VulnFinding>. ~700 LOC of CLI-local code.
tldr_vuln_command_flow_today.4_rust_path: analyze_rust_file at L747 — pure line-scanner for unsafe blocks, raw pointers, transmute, unwrap-in-non-test, mysql_query, etc. ~250 LOC. NOT taint-flow analysis — it's pattern-detector for Rust-idiom security smells. SCOPE QUESTION: does this migrate? See plan §1.
tldr_vuln_command_flow_today.5_other_14_path: scan_vulnerabilities → scan_file_vulns at vuln.rs:903 — PURE substring scanner. line.contains(source_pattern), line.contains(sink_pattern), line.contains(var). NO AST awareness. THIS is the FP-prone path.

fp_repro_at_head.fixture: /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.go — Go file with substring 'os.Args' inside a string literal at line 12 and the substring 'mentions os.Args' at line 27. Sink lines exec.Command(cmd) at L19 and exec.Command(user_input) at L28.
fp_repro_at_head.result_at_head_c842962: 3 FALSE POSITIVE CommandInjection findings emitted. Each cites a string-literal source line as the taint source. Exit code 2 (vulnerabilities detected) — the binary signals to a CI consumer that 3 critical vulns exist when zero do.
fp_repro_at_head.ts_repro: Confirmed: /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/fp2.ts with 'req.query' substring inside string literal AND 'docs' variable assignment → 1 FP CommandInjection. Even more egregiously, the cited 'sink' line is a COMMENT (the // line at 5 'on line with 'child_process.exec(', see line.contains('docs') → fire.') — the substring scanner does not strip comments.
fp_repro_at_head.py_negative_control: /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.py — same string-literal pattern with 'request.args' substring + os.system call → ZERO findings (Python's tree-sitter analyze_python_file path is AST-aware; assignments from string literals do not register as taint sources because is_taint_source(rhs_text) returns None on string literals).
fp_repro_at_head.verdict: FP class is concentrated in the 14 substring-fall-through languages routed via core scan_vulnerabilities. Python AST path is FP-clean. Rust line-scanner path covers a different concern (unsafe-pattern detection, not taint flow) so the FP class doesn't apply.

regex_bank_assertion_tests: test_get_sources_python (L1322) — asserts get_sources(Python) returns non-empty. Bank-content test. POST-MIGRATION: keep IF banks retained as fallback; delete IF banks removed.
test_get_sinks_sql_injection (L1329) — same pattern. KEEP/DELETE same as above.
core_vuln_inline_tests_at_vuln_rs_L1322_.verdict: E2E tests are KEEP — they assert behavior end-to-end, NOT bank content. They will be the primary regression guard during migration. The bank-assertion tests (~5) become obsolete if banks are deleted.

cli_integration_tests.vuln_autodetect_tests_rs: 6 tests — autodetect routing + CLI args. KEEP (asserts CLI behavior, not internals).
cli_integration_tests.verdict: All CLI tests preserved — they assert user-facing CLI behavior.

cli_local_unit_tests_at_remaining_vuln_r: test_is_taint_source (L1844) — asserts CLI-local PYTHON_SOURCES const. Bank-content. DELETE if Python path migrates.
test_taint_tracker (L1900) — asserts CLI-local TaintTracker. DELETE if migrates.
test_string_interpolation_detection — DELETE if string-interpolation routes through canonical AST.
test_collect_files_includes_rust + 6 test_analyze_rust_* — KEEP (Rust line-scanner is not in scope for canonical-taint migration; see plan §1).

scope_decision_rust_line_scanner.question: Does analyze_rust_file (the line-scanner for unsafe / transmute / raw-pointer / unwrap-in-non-test / mysql_query) migrate to canonical compute_taint_with_tree?
scope_decision_rust_line_scanner.decision: NO — out of scope. analyze_rust_file detects Rust-IDIOMATIC security smells (UnsafeCode, MemorySafety, Panic vuln types from cli types::VulnType), NOT taint flows from sources to sinks. compute_taint_with_tree is a CFG-based source-to-sink propagation engine; it is not the right tool for 'detect unwrap() in non-test code' or 'detect transmute() usage'. These are smells, not taint flows. They would need a DIFFERENT canonical analysis (which doesn't exist today).
scope_decision_rust_line_scanner.implication: The Rust path stays as a line-scanner. The migration is about taint-flow detection ONLY (the 5 taint-flow VulnTypes: SqlInjection, Xss, CommandInjection, PathTraversal, Ssrf, Deserialization). UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic remain analyze_rust_file's responsibility. This narrows the migration scope and avoids architectural overreach.

predecessor_milestones_status.regex_removal_v1: Removed regex source/sink banks for 13 GO languages from compute_taint_with_tree path.
predecessor_milestones_status.field_access_info_extension_v1: Added structured AST field-access info; deleted Ruby/Elixir/OCaml source+sink regex banks from compute_taint path.
predecessor_milestones_status.sanitizer_removal_v1: Wired detect_sanitizer_ast through compute_taint_with_tree; deleted 30 regex sanitizer Vec entries; eliminated string-literal substring FP class for SANITIZERS.
predecessor_milestones_status.all_three_above_only_touch: tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs — i.e., the `tldr taint` command path. NONE reach `tldr vuln`.
predecessor_milestones_status.carry_forward_to_this_milestone: Ruby \bgets\b regex source pattern (RETAINED in regex-removal-v1 because tree-sitter-ruby parses bare `gets` as identifier not call). Will need re-evaluation if vuln.rs's Ruby source bank uses bare `STDIN.gets` matching.

external_consumer_risk_assessment.json_output_shape: tldr vuln --format json emits JSON conforming to CLI types::VulnReport (NOT core's VulnReport). All shape fields preserved by migration as long as the CLI's analyze_file → VulnFinding construction site is updated to read from canonical TaintInfo instead of core's VulnReport. SARIF output is built from same VulnFinding via generate_sarif at remaining/vuln.rs:~1700.
external_consumer_risk_assessment.exit_code: Exit 2 on findings detected (per RemainingError::findings_detected). Preserved.
external_consumer_risk_assessment.cli_args: All clap-derived flags preserved. No CLI surface change.
external_consumer_risk_assessment.performance: compute_taint_with_tree per-function with cfg + dfg + ssa construction is HEAVIER than substring scanning per-line. Per-file overhead increases. MITIGATION: parallelize per-file (rayon) or cache parsed trees. To validate, add a microbenchmark to the M1 RED phase.
external_consumer_risk_assessment.verdict: LOW external-consumer risk. Output shape preserved; only internal pipeline changes. Performance regression is the primary concern — MEDIUM risk; mitigated by per-file parallelism.

new_variants_proposed.name: HtmlOutput
new_variants_proposed.patterns_per_lang: innerHTML, document.write, Markup(), html_safe, raw(), Html.Raw — already present in vuln.rs:381-427

new_variants_proposed.name: FileOpen
new_variants_proposed.patterns_per_lang: open(), Path(), os.path.join, File.read, File.write — already present in vuln.rs:513-608. Note: distinct from existing FileWrite which is narrower (write-only).

new_variants_proposed.name: HttpRequest
new_variants_proposed.patterns_per_lang: requests.get, fetch, axios.get, http.Get, URL.openConnection — already present in vuln.rs:609-711

new_variants_proposed.name: Deserialize
new_variants_proposed.patterns_per_lang: pickle.load, yaml.load, ObjectInputStream.readObject, Marshal.load, unserialize — already present in vuln.rs:712-779

extension_required_taintsinktype.verdict: Additive enum extension — preserves backward compat for all existing TaintSinkType consumers (existing variants unchanged). New variants integrate via standard match-arm extension. Sink AST patterns must be added to all 16 LanguagePatterns AST sink banks, mirroring vuln.rs's existing per-language tables.

loc_estimate_summary.m1_red_tests_plus_taintsinktype_extensio: +250 LOC (2 string-literal + 2 real-flow fixtures × 14 languages × 6 vuln_types = ~120 tests; TaintSinkType +4 variants + AST-bank entries ~50 LOC)
loc_estimate_summary.m2_collapse_core_vuln_rs_types_to_canoni: -150 LOC net (delete 4 duplicate types, replace map_core_vuln_type, retain VulnType+VulnFinding output records as wrappers)
loc_estimate_summary.m3_route_through_compute_taint_with_tree: -450 LOC net (delete scan_file_vulns inline propagation; replace with per-function cfg+dfg+ssa+compute_taint loop wrapping the result into VulnFinding records)
loc_estimate_summary.m4_collapse_cli_python_path_onto_canonic: -650 LOC net (delete CLI's TaintSource/TaintSink const tables + TaintTracker + analyze_python_file's tree-sitter walk; replace with canonical compute_taint_with_tree call analogous to remaining/taint.rs:128)
loc_estimate_summary.m5_atomic_delete_obsolete_tests_plus_dis: -200 LOC (delete ~15 bank-assertion unit tests + the now-dead get_sources/get_sinks per-language tables)
loc_estimate_summary.net_total_estimate: approximately -1170 LOC net deletion (main pipeline) + 250 LOC of new RED tests + ~50 LOC of new TaintSinkType variant additions + AST sink bank entries

open_questions_for_executor: Q1: Should core's vuln.rs be DELETED entirely (replaced by a thin CLI-side wrapper around compute_taint_with_tree) or COLLAPSED IN PLACE (retain scan_vulnerabilities public API as a thin wrapper)? Recommendation: collapse-in-place to preserve external library callers of tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities. Audit: the only in-tree caller is remaining/vuln.rs:654 — but the function is exported via mod.rs:25 (pub use vuln::scan_vulnerabilities) which means external lib consumers may exist.

Q2: Should the CLI-local PYTHON_SOURCES/PYTHON_SINKS const tables be migrated to canonical AST source/sink banks at taint.rs:1700+, or merged inline into the migration? Recommendation: merge inline — taint.rs's PYTHON_AST_SOURCES already has the full source set. The CLI's tables duplicate these. Investigate parity to confirm before deleting.

Q3: For Java's UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic VulnTypes — does any path in core/vuln.rs or CLI/vuln.rs emit them? Audit: only analyze_rust_file emits these. Confirmed scope: Rust line-scanner stays separate.

Q4: Performance budget — what's the acceptable per-file slowdown for migrating from substring scan to per-function CFG+DFG+SSA+compute_taint? Recommendation: define a stop_threshold in M3 — e.g., a 20-file Go fixture must run in < 2× the pre-migration wall-clock time.
verdict: READY FOR PLAN — investigation surfaces 2 architectural reframes that the plan must address: (a) duplicate types span BOTH core/vuln.rs AND cli/vuln.rs (not just one); (b) canonical TaintSinkType requires +4 variants before vuln.rs can be cleanly collapsed onto it. Recommend running discriminative premortem before /autonomous to surface any additional gaps. FP repro is concrete and reproducible; tests + fixtures are well-scoped.
